title: TikTok Video Editor Ascend Athlete
description:
WHO WE ARE



We are Kai and Miko Oppenheim — two brothers who both 

play Division 1 soccer. We built Ascend, a 6-pillar performance system for 

youth soccer athletes. 10 athletes committed D1 this 

year using our program.



Our TikTok teaches real things: 

nutrition, mental performance, tactical IQ, recruiting, 

technical skills, and recovery. We are not a highlight 

account. We have a story and a system and the content 

reflects both.



We have a complete professionally written Content Bible 

that tells you exactly what to edit, how to edit it, 

what text overlays to add at what timestamps, and what 

each video needs to do algorithmically. Your job is to 

execute it precisely — not to guess.



WHAT THE ROLE ACTUALLY IS



This is not an "edit whatever looks cool" role. The 

Content Bible specifies the exact CapCut edit sequence 

for every video, the exact text overlays with timestamps 

and font sizes, the footage scoring system before 

editing, the color grade preset to apply, and a 

pre-handoff checklist before you upload anything.



We post 6 videos per week. You deliver all 6 together 

by Saturday of the prior week — one batch, one review 

session.



CONTENT TYPES YOU WILL EDIT



Skill breakdowns — show result first with no text for 

4 seconds, 3-step breakdown, slow-mo replay at the end.



Education videos — text-overlay driven, 45-55 seconds, 

specific label colors and pacing.



Origin and story videos — emotional arc, precise text 

timing, warm color grade.



ASMR videos — zero music, natural sounds raised to 

130-150% volume, 18-26 seconds maximum only.



Proof and sell videos — testimonial quotes at large 

font, names list, direct call-to-action sequence.



WHAT YOU MUST BE ABLE TO DO



CapCut is the primary tool. You must be able to:

- Apply a saved color grade preset to all clips

- Sync audio to visuals using the waveform panel

- Add precise text overlays at exact timestamps with 

  correct font and color

- Apply slow-motion using Speed Curve Hero preset

- Export at 1080x1920 30fps H.264 high quality

- Use hard cuts only — no transition effects ever



You must communicate clearly in English, flag missing 

footage before editing, and deliver on time.



WHAT YOU DO NOT DO



You never post to TikTok — we handle posting entirely.

You never add music — we add music before posting.

You never rewrite captions — copy-paste only from bible.

You never share footage outside the project.



THE WORKFLOW



We upload raw footage to Google Drive on Wednesday. 

You score every clip, edit all 6 videos following the 

Content Bible, upload to Frame.io, and send one message 

when all 6 are ready. We review and leave timestamp 

comments. You make changes. Done for the week.



PAY



Paid per video. Rate based on your trial edit quality. 

We start with one paid trial task. If the trial is 

strong we move to a weekly agreement. We are looking 

for someone long-term.



HOW TO APPLY



Send the following:



1. Short introduction — who you are, where you are 

from, how long you have been editing short-form video.



2. Links to 3-5 videos you edited yourself. TikTok, 

Reels, or Shorts.



3. One example of following precise editing 

instructions — describe what the brief was and how 

you executed it.



4. Your CapCut skill level — beginner, intermediate, 

or advanced.



5. Your typical turnaround time for a 60-second edit.



6. Include the word "Oppenheim" in your application. 

Applications without this word will not be reviewed.



SECOND ROUND



Shortlisted applicants will complete a paid trial edit 

using one real brief from our Content Bible. We provide 

footage and exact instructions. Deliver within 48 hours. 

We pay for this regardless of whether we hire you.



We are not looking for the most creative editor. We are 

looking for the most precise and reliable one.

title: Remote Digital & Operations Assistant - Full Time
description:
Remote Digital & Operations Assistant — Full Time

We’re an online art gallery platform based in the UK, approaching our 22nd year. We run multiple websites, a Shopify store, and are actively expanding into new territories and product lines. It’s a busy, creative environment and there’s a lot to do.

We’re looking for a full-time remote assistant who is technically confident, highly organised, and can work independently once briefed. You’ll be the person who makes things happen while I focus on the bigger picture.

You’ll be working across:

 • Shopify — product listings, supplier accounts, app setup and management, store maintenance and build

 • Wix — content updates and management across multiple sites

 •
 correspondence — supplier follow-ups, customer queries, outreach
s

You’ll have clear direction and real oversight — but between calls, I need you to get on with it independently and come back with things done.

title: GOOGLE ADS STRATEGIST ( Remote )
description:
We are not looking for someone to simply manage a budget. We are looking for a performance architect who views Google Ads as a precision instrument, not a gambling machine. If you are tired of corporate templates and want a role where your technical expertise directly dictates the growth of a business, read on.



The Objective

Your mission is to go beyond clicks and impressions. You will be responsible for the entire conversion ecosystem. We need a professional who can diagnose a dropping ROAS in minutes and pivot strategies before the day is done. You will own the strategy, the execution, and the results.



What You Will Actually Do

Engineer and scale complex accounts using Search, Shopping, YouTube, and Performance Max.



Deconstruct user behavior through Google Analytics 4 and Tag Manager to find the "why" behind the data.



A/B test everything—from ad copy and bidding strategies to Landing Page friction points.



Aggressively optimize for CPA and ROAS, ensuring every dollar spent is an investment, not an expense.



Work with a high degree of autonomy, making real-time adjustments based on market shifts.



The Standard We Require

Direct, hands-on experience managing high-spend accounts. We value your track record over your degree.



A deep understanding of Google’s algorithm shifts and a proactive approach to the transition toward automation and AI-driven bidding.



Expertise in attribution modeling and cross-channel tracking.



The ability to explain complex data in simple, actionable business insights.



A mindset that hates "average" performance and thrives on constant improvement.



The Partnership

We offer a high-trust environment. There is no micromanagement here, only a focus on results. You will have the tools, the budget, and the creative freedom to execute your vision. Compensation is competitive and designed to reward those who consistently hit or exceed their targets.



How to Apply

Skip the generic cover letter. Send your CV and a brief summary of your most significant performance win (the challenge, your specific intervention, and the final metrics) to:







Subject Line: [Google Ads Application] - [Full Name] - [Years of Experience]



We are looking for a partner to help us grow. If you have the data to prove you are that person, we want to hear from you.

Edit 1–5 minute video ads for
/Instagram — direct response, not brand fluff. You'll work from scripts we provide but you're expected to bring your own creative input. Specifically:



• AI-generated video using tools like Kling, Veo 3, Runway, etc. — you should already know these or be the type who figures them out fast

• Pixar-style animated ad creatives (AI-generated characters, scenes, storytelling)

• Mixed format ads combining AI-generated footage with sourced B-roll

• Multiple hook variations per concept

• Text overlays, captions, sound design, pacing for retention



You must:



• Be obsessed with staying current on AI video tools — if a new tool drops, you should know about it before we do

• Be fast and resourceful at finding high-quality B-roll and stock footage

• Understand direct-response ad pacing — hooks, retention, CTR

• Deliver clean work, fast. We need volume and quality

• Communicate clearly — ask questions before you start, not after you deliver

• Be proficient in Premiere Pro, CapCut, or both. After Effects is a plus



Paid test task (required):



We'll send you a product and script. You create 1–2 edited videos with 3 hook variations each. You source the footage, music, and handle all editing. This is how we hire — no exceptions.



What you get:



• Stable long-term work, not a gig

• Room to grow into a lead creative role

• We pay properly and on time



To apply, send:



• Short intro + your experience with AI video tools specifically

• 2–3 ad samples you've edited (e-commerce preferred)

• Your software stack

• Availability (hours/day + timezone)

• Confirmation you'll do the paid test task



Don't apply if you're not willing to learn new tools every month. This space moves fast and we need someone who moves with it.

title: Customer Service VA — E-Commerce (Dropshipping
description:
**Schedule:** Monday–Saturday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (US Eastern Time)

**Type:** Full-time, Remote



---



## About the Role



We're looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to manage day-to-day customer service and operations across our e-commerce stores. You'll be the first point of contact for customers and a right hand for the business owner — handling everything from inbox management to keeping our social media channels clean and responsive.



This isn't a "sit around and wait for tickets" role. You'll need to be proactive, fast on your feet, and comfortable juggling multiple platforms at once.



## What You'll Be Doing



**Customer Service & Communication**

- Respond to customer
s promptly — target response time is under 1 hour during working hours

- Handle
 and Instagram 
 across all store pages

- Monitor and manage comments on our
 and Instagram pages (respond, hide, or escalate as needed)

- Process refund requests, order issues, and shipping inquiries

- Maintain a professional, friendly tone that reflects the brand



**Store & Operations Support**

- Assist with general store management tasks (product listings, order tracking, inventory checks)

- Help with ad-hoc tasks as they come up — you'll be working directly with the store owner

- Flag anything unusual: chargebacks, complaint patterns, stock issues, etc.

- Keep organized records and follow up on open issues without being reminded



## What We're Looking For



- Previous experience as a VA or in e-commerce customer service (dropshipping experience is a strong plus)

- Excellent written English — clear, professional, no copy-paste robotic replies

- Comfortable working across
,
, and Instagram simultaneously

- Able to work full US hours: **9 AM – 5 PM EST, Monday through Friday** — this is non-negotiable

- Fast learner who can pick up new tools, platforms, and SOPs quickly

- Self-starter who doesn't need to be micromanaged

- Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace



## Bonus Points



- Experience with Shopify

- Familiarity with Meta Business Suite

- Experience with subscription-based or DTC (direct-to-consumer) e-commerce

- Familiarity with handling chargebacks, refund flows, and subscription cancellations



## To Apply



Send a short message including:

1. A brief intro about yourself and your relevant experience

2. Your timezone and confirmation you can work 9 AM – 5 PM EST

3. Tools/platforms you've used (Shopify, Zendesk, Freshdesk, Meta Business Suite, etc.)

4. Your expected weekly rate (USD)

title: Social Media Specialist
description:
Quick question!

Are you tired of working as an average Social Media Manager, never growing professionally, and never getting the chance to take your career to the next level?

Do you want to experience working with clients on a larger platform? Do you want to learn the skills required to help US-based business owners, influencers, and public figures take their social media presence and personal brands to the level of virality?



WE ARE HIRING!

We are looking for individuals who are eager to learn and want to take their digital marketing careers to new heights.

Enjoy benefits such as:

Unlimited access to almost $10,000 worth of training and courses for both personal skills and development.

The chance to work with clients that will elevate your resume, giving you the opportunity to break into the international market.

Working alongside a team that prioritizes a healthy work environment.

An environment that cultivates personal growth, serving as the foundation for skill development.





Just Simply Marketing (JSM) is a digital marketing agency based in Ontario, Canada, dedicated to helping business coaches, speakers, and experts from the US and Canada build, grow, and monetize their online brands. Specializing in organic marketing strategies, we focus on content creation, social media management, lead generation, and video production to help our clients stand out online. At JSM, we value creativity, excellence, and personal growth in everything we do.

You will work closely with our creatives, video editing, and graphic design teams to review and approve videos and designs, ensuring they align with our clients' brand and strategy. As our Social Media Specialist, you will be the driving force behind our clients' brand growth and increased online presence.

This is a fully remote position with working hours from 6:00 AM to 2:00 PM EST, Monday to Friday. The Social Media Specialist will report directly to the Social Media Team Managers.



How to Apply:

Interested candidates, please apply through our application form: 



RESPONSIBILITIES:

-Content Research: Use our FREE AI & Software tools to develop effective social media strategies.

-Content Creation: You will be responsible for creating content ideas to grow your client’s social media presence, build their personal brand, and ultimately help them generate income through their social media platforms. Don’t worry, you don’t have to edit anything! You’ll be working alongside a video editing and graphic design team to create content!

-Copywriting & Scriptwriting: You will work hand-in-hand with a team and your client to build effective storytelling copy for social media. We will train you to use AI tools and storytelling strategies to captivate an audience.

-Content Posting: You will handle the scheduling and posting of content across various social media platforms in line with the content calendar and strategy you've created.

-Content Analysis and Reporting: You will generate reports on account performance and audience engagement. These insights will be used to optimize strategies and create better content for our clients.



ALL TRAINING PROVIDED – WORTH $10,000 – IS FREE



REQUIREMENTS:

-A minimum of 1 year of experience as a social media manager.

-Proficient in using Instagram,
, TikTok, and YouTube.

-Proven track record in achieving results for your past/current clients' online brands.

-Experience in copywriting and understanding of SEO & keywords.

-Looking to work for the long term and grow within the company.

-Experience with 
, Metricool, Slack, Google Drive, and Google Docs is highly preferred but not required.

-Strong communication skills – Confidence is a PLUS as you will be consistently interacting with US/Canada-based clients.



P.S. Don’t worry if you do not meet all the requirements, we have the training to help you!



How to Apply:

If this sounds like the perfect fit for you, please fill out our application form:

title: Content Creator with Adobe & GHL skills
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled Content Creator and Digital Marketing VA to support our growing UK-based marketing and consultancy agency. This is a multifaceted role requiring a blend of technical high-end visual content creation, CRM management, and administrative excellence.



ESSENTIAL SKILLS - Please do not apply if you do not match these specific criteria:

# English Proficiency: An excellent, provable C1 / C2 level of English. This must be a formal certification (e.g., IELTS, Cambridge, TOEFL); we cannot accept results from free online quizzes.

# GoHighLevel (GHL): Minimum 6 months of experience. You must be proficient in automations, sites, funnels, and general CRM management.

# Adobe Premiere Pro: Proficiency is essential. Note: You must have your own active Adobe subscription.

# Design Tools: Intermediate level in Adobe Photoshop (own subscription required) OR Canva.

# Social Media Marketing: Deep knowledge of best practices and trends for LinkedIn, Instagram, and
.

# Lead Generation: Experience with tools such as LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

# Client Communication: Confident, fluent video-conferencing skills for client-facing work.

# General VA Skills: High-level organizational skills and reliability.



DESIRABLE SKILLS - These are considered a bonus only if ALL essential skills are met:

# API & Automation: Knowledge of Make.com and n8n.

# AI Tools: Proficiency in GPT, Gemini, and Claude (utilizing AI to enhance workflow without being dependent on it).

# Web Development: Knowledge of Elementor, WordPress, and intermediate CSS.

# Audio/Video Tools: Experience with Descript.



ROLE DETAILS & REQUIREMENTS

# Hours: Starting at 10 hours per week, with the intention of moving to a full-time position for the right candidate.

# Portfolio: We require a comprehensive portfolio demonstrating your video editing and design work.

# Assessment: Please note there is a live skills assessment as part of the interview process.

# Verification: You will be required to provide proof of your English certification.



Who we are looking for:

We are a friendly and flexible agency, but we move fast. We need someone dependable, communicative, and ready to take ownership of content and systems.



STRICT REQUIREMENT: Do not apply if you have less than 6 months of GoHighLevel experience or if you do not hold a formal C1/C2 English certification.



Please send a link to your portfolio and we'd be happy to review it.  Ensure it has examples of your work in Adobe Photoshop and Premiere.

title: AI Systems Specialist
description:
ABOUT THE SOURCING CO (TSC)



TSC is a fast-growing multi-brand sourcing and supply business headquartered in Guangzhou, China, with operations across Australia, Papua New Guinea, and the Pacific. We work across apparel, building materials, corporate merchandise, modular housing, and more. We are a real business with a 10-year track record - not a startup, not a corporation.



We are now building out our AI infrastructure and are looking for the right person to lead it.



---



THE ROLE



Job Title: AI Systems Specialist

Type: Full-time

Location: Remote (Philippines-based preferred)

Reports to: Group GM and Managing Director

Salary: USD 1,500 - 2,000/month depending on experience

Travel: Quarterly trips to Guangzhou, China (approx. 1 month per quarter, fully covered)



---



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING



AI & Automation

- Build and deploy AI agents and automated workflow systems using tools like n8n, Zapier, Make, or equivalent

- Research, trial, and implement the best available AI tools for specific business use cases

- Integrate AI into day-to-day business operations to make the team faster and more productive

- Build bulk personalised communication systems (e.g. client onboarding, login links, campaign triggers)



Web Development

- Design and develop functional, well-presented websites for TSC Group brands

- Build e-commerce and branded retail sites for sub-brands

- Use AI-assisted coding tools to accelerate development without sacrificing quality



Data Handling

- Extract, clean, and migrate data from existing databases and tools into websites and CRMs

- Build lightweight data pipelines that keep business information accurate across systems



On-Site Implementation

- Travel to Guangzhou quarterly to oversee AI systems implementation on the ground

- Work directly with the MD and relevant teams during visits to test, refine, and embed new systems

- Document systems clearly so non-technical tea
mbers can operate them independently



---



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



- Deep, practical experience with Claude (Anthropic) and OpenAI APIs - actual integration and agent development, not just chat tools

- Proven track record building AI agents and automation workflows

- Strong web development skills - you can build attractive, functional websites from scratch

- Experience with e-commerce platforms (Shopify, WooCommerce, or equivalent) is a plus

- Able to write, generate, and debug code - Python preferred

- Comfortable working with APIs, webhooks, and data integrations

- An eye for design - you care how things look, not just how they work



---



WHO YOU ARE



- Genuinely obsessed with AI - you follow the space closely, experiment constantly, and implement new tools before most people have heard of them

- Self-directed - you see a problem and propose a solution without being asked

- Clear communicator - you can explain what you're building to a non-technical MD

- Commercially minded - every system you build exists to make the business faster or more profitable

- Willing and able to travel internationally once per quarter (valid passport required)



---



WHY THIS ROLE



You will report directly to the Group GM and MD, work on real problems across a multi-brand, fast-growing group, and have the freedom to build AI systems that actually get used. If you want a role where your work matters from day one - this is it.



To apply, please share your portfolio or examples of AI agents, automation workflows, or websites you have built.

title: Experienced clipper & distributor
description:
AI-Powered Content Clipper & Distribution Strategist — 7-Figure Brand, Daily Posting

Full-time or part-time, ongoing

Rate: open to your proposal

Who I Am

I’m Chelsea, founder of Organic Product Launch / Learn With Chelsea — a personal + digital product brand that’s taught 20,000+ entrepreneurs. I have a low-ticket funnel, a free AI-powered lead magnet, and a library of raw content (Reels, livestreams, podcast-style videos, long-form teaching). What I don’t have is enough hours in the day to turn that content into a daily distribution machine across every platform.

That’s where you come in.

What You’ll Actually Do

You’re not just a clipper. You’re the operator who turns my raw content into a daily traffic engine that grows my list and drives low-ticket sales.

Daily output:

 • 3–5 posts per page, per day, across 3–4 pages (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts,
 — we’ll align on the exact mix)

 • That’s 3-5 polished, platform-optimized posts every day

The work includes:

 • Watching raw long-form content and identifying the viral 
nts (hook, story, teaching beat, controversial take)

 • Clipping, editing, and captioning in your tool of choice (CapCut, Descript, Opus Clip, Submagic — whatever gets results)

 • Writing scroll-stopping on-screen text and captions that match my brand voice (vulnerability + empowerment, no fluff)

 • Embedding strategic CTAs that grow my
 list via my AI freebie OR push my low-ticket digital products

 • Cross-platform formatting (9:16, captions burned in, platform-native hooks)

 • Posting or scheduling across all pages (or handing off in a tight workflow — your call)

 • Tracking what hits and doubling down on winning formats

Who You Are

 • AI-fluent, Claude-first. You use Claude daily for hook-writing, caption drafting, identifying clip-worthy 
nts from transcripts, and brainstorming variations. If you’ve never used Claude, this isn’t your role.

 • Proficient in AI clipping tools — Opus Clip, Submagic, Descript, Vizard, or similar. You know which tool to use for which job.

 • Strategic, not just creative. You understand the difference between a clip that gets views and a clip that grows a list or sells a product. Both matter.

 • You’ve clipped for a creator or brand before — ideally in the online business, digital products, or creator-economy space

 • Fast without being sloppy. Daily volume at this level requires real systems.

 • You have opinions. If a clip isn’t working, you flag it. If you see a better angle in my raw content, you pitch it. I don’t want a passive editor.

 • You study what’s working. You know who’s winning on short-form right now and why.

Who You Are NOT

 • A traditional video editor looking for “edit this one YouTube video” jobs

 • Someone who needs a shot-list and script for every clip

 • AI-curious but not AI-fluent — this role requires real proficiency, not Googling “how to use ChatGPT”

 • Someone who’ll post and ghost — I need a partner watching the numbers

Tools You’ll Likely Use

Claude, CapCut or Descript, Opus Clip or Submagic, Metricool or Later for scheduling, Google Drive for raw content handoff, Notion or Trello for tracking. If you have a better stack, pitch it.

How to Apply

Applications missing any of these will get skipped:

 1. Send me 3 clips you’ve made — ideally from a piece of raw long-form content you can also link to (so I can see your eye for picking 
nts). Short-form clips for a real creator/brand are best.

 2. Answer in 3–5 sentences: “I hand you a 45-minute livestream where I teach how to pick a profitable niche. Walk me through your process — from first watch to published clips across 3 platforms. What does your workflow look like and what AI tools do you use where?”

 3. Tell me how you use Claude specifically. What prompts, what tasks, what results? Share a real example.

 4. Your proposed rate (per-post, weekly, or monthly — your preference) and your availability to start in the next 2 weeks.



Put the phrase “clip strategist” in the first line of your application so I know you read this.

If this works, there’s serious long-term growth here — more pages, a bigger content engine, and room to lead a small content team down the line.

— Chelsea

title: Paid Media Specialist (Google & Meta Ads)
description:

 & Instagram) platforms.

This role plays a key part in driving qualified leads, customer acquisition, and revenue growth through strategic planning, hands-on execution, and ongoing performance optimization.



II. Key Responsibilities

Plan, execute, and manage paid media campaigns across Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max) and Meta Ads platforms.

IV. Preferred Qualifications

V. What We Offer

Clear career progression pathways.

title: Content & Influencer Manager
description:
About Us:

We’re a fast-scaling DTC supplement brand with a high-output creative operation. We move fast, we care deeply about creative quality, and we’re building a team of people who are genuinely obsessed with content — not just managing it.



The Role:

We’re looking for a full-time Content & Partnerships Manager to sit at the intersection of creator management, influencer partnerships, and organic content strategy. You’ll work hand-in-hand with our Creative Strategist to bring content ideas to life — from initial brief to final deliverable.



This isn’t a “schedule posts and write captions” role. This is for someone who lives and breathes content, knows what’s trending before it peaks, and gets real satisfaction from seeing a creative idea execute perfectly.



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Briefing content creators — translating creative strategies into clear, actionable briefs covering B-roll shot lists, talking head scripts, testimonial formats, and original content concepts

- Managing creator relationships — owning the full workflow from outreach and onboarding to deliverable tracking and content labeling

- Managing influencer & brand partnerships — identifying, vetting, and managing ongoing partnerships that align with our brand

- Collaborating with our Creative Strategist — receiving creative direction and ensuring it gets executed accurately and on time by the right creators

- Staying ahead of trends — you should have a genuine pulse on what’s working on TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts right now



You’re the Right Fit If…

- You’re organically obsessed — you scroll TikTok and think “we should do that” before you think anything else

- You’ve managed UGC creators or influencers before and know how to get great output from them

- You can read a creative strategy and immediately understand what the content should look and feel like

- You’re organized enough to manage multiple creators and deadlines without things slipping

Bonus: you’ve grown or managed an organic social presence for a brand and can point to real results



Nice to Haves (Big Bonus):

- Experience with TikTok organic growth for a DTC or e-commerce brand

- You’ve run or been deeply involved in a UGC / creator program

- You think in content ideas naturally — you’re always noticing hooks, angles, and formats in the wild



What We’re NOT Looking For

- Someone who just schedules content and calls it management

- Someone who needs every idea handed to them

- Someone who isn’t genuinely excited by creative content



To Apply

Tell us in your proposal:

- A brand (DTC or otherwise) whose organic content you think is exceptional right now — and why

- A brief example of how you’d brief a creator on a B-roll concept for a health/wellness product

- Your experience managing influencers or UGC creators

title: Email marketing strategist + copywriter
description:
Who I Am

I’m Chelsea, founder of Organic Product Launch / Learn With Chelsea — a personal brand + digital product brand serving 20,000+ entrepreneurs. I have a low-ticket funnel ecosystem, a high-ticket DFY backend, an active newsletter, and post-purchase sequences that need to be sharper, more strategic, and more profitable.

I’m looking for one person to own
 end-to-end: strategy, copy, logic, deployment, and optimization. If you love the game of turning a list into a revenue engine, this is your role.

The Compensation Structure

 • Monthly base pay — we’ll align based on your experience and proposal

 • 10% commission on all
-generated sales (tracked via UTMs, Hyros, and GHL attribution)

 • Real upside for someone who treats my list like their own

The better you are, the more you earn. I want a partner, not a button-pusher.

What You’ll Own

Strategy & Research

 • Study my top competitors (I’ll share who) and model what’s working — hook patterns, sequence structures, offer angles, subject line formulas

 • Bring back insights, not just copies. I want a strategist who reverse-engineers why something converts, then adapts it to my brand voice.

 • Identify gaps in my current
 flows and pitch what should be built next

Copy

 • Write the weekly newsletter in my voice (vulnerability + empowerment, no fluff, no corporate)

 • Write and optimize post-purchase sequences for every offer in my value ladder

 • Cross-promote offers strategically — $27 buyers nurtured toward $47, $47 buyers toward $97, $97 buyers toward DFY

 • Write abandoned cart, re-engagement, flash sale, and VIP buyer sequences

 • Craft subject lines and preview text that actually get opened

GHL Workflow & Logic

 • Build and maintain
 workflows inside GoHighLevel. These have already been created. 

 • Design tag-based segmentation so buyers, non-buyers, and different buyer tiers get the right message at the right time

 • on any new
s you create- Set conditional logic (opened vs. didn’t, clicked vs. didn’t, purchased vs. abandoned) to avoid generic blasts

 • Coordinate with my GHL workflow architect so
 logic plays cleanly with the broader funnel

Optimization

 • Track opens, clicks, conversions, and revenue per

 • A/B test subject lines, hooks, CTAs, and send times

 • Kill what doesn’t work, scale what does, and report results monthly

Who You Are

 • 3+ years writing
 copy for offers that actually sell (digital products, courses, info products, or DTC — not just SaaS newsletters)

 • Proven conversion results you can show — open rates, click rates, and revenue numbers from real campaigns

 • GHL proficient — you know how to build workflows, use tags, set conditions, and segment lists. Not just “I’ve seen GHL.” I mean you can architect inside it.

 • AI-fluent, chat gpt or Claude-first. You use ai daily for research, drafting, variant generation, and subject line testing. This is non-negotiable — the role requires the speed and leverage AI gives you.

 • Strategic copywriter, not just a good writer. You understand buyer psychology, awareness levels, and how to move someone from cold to sold across a sequence.

 • You write in the brand voice, not yours. You can study my existing content and disappear into my tone.

 • You think in sequences, not one-offs. Every
 serves a larger arc.

 • Data-literate. You read
 metrics and adjust. You don’t send and forget.

Who You Are NOT

 • A generalist copywriter who “also does
”

 • Someone who needs me to hand you a brief for every send

 • An AI-curious writer who uses ChatGPT occasionally — this role demands real AI fluency

 • Someone looking for base pay without caring about the commission (that tells me everything)

 • A “set it and forget it”
 marketer — I need ongoing strategy, not a one-time build

Tools You’ll Use

GoHighLevel 
 + workflows), Claude, Hyros for attribution, Deadline Funnel for flash sales, Google Drive, Notion or Trello for campaign tracking. If you have a better stack recommendation, pitch it.

How to Apply

Applications missing any of these get skipped:

 1. Send 2–3
 campaigns you’ve written that generated measurable revenue. Include the subject line, the body, and the results (open rate, click rate, revenue if possible).

 2. Answer in 5–7 sentences: “I sell a $27 order bump, a $47 standalone product, a $97 upsell, and a high-ticket DFY service. A new buyer just purchased the $27 order bump. Walk me through the post-purchase
 sequence you’d build over the next 14 days — how many
s, what angles, what CTAs, and how you’d use GHL tags and logic to route them based on behavior.”

 3. Tell me how you use ai in your
 workflow. Specific prompts, specific tasks, specific wins. Generic answers tell me you don’t actually use it.

 4. Propose your compensation: your monthly base pay expectation + confirmation that 10% commission on
-generated sales works for you. Include your availability to start.



This is a long-term role for the right person. As revenue grows, so does your cut.

— Chelsea

title: Elite AI Animation Ad Creator — Pure AI Animation Only

title: Video Editor (PHP 358/HR - USD $6/HR | PART Time)
description:
Hey!



We are looking for a Video Editor ROCKSTAR who will turn our long form content into high-performing short form clips and weekly YouTube videos.



We are a marketing and education company that works with English speakers and already have a team of high-performing individuals from the Philippines. Mastery of the English language is a requirement and will be tested prior to hiring.



BENEFITS INCLUDE:

- 100% remote

- Weekly pay

- USD $6 / Hour = 355 PHP / Hour

- Paid training

- No work during the weekends

- Opportunity to go from part time to full time

- We pay for the AI and creative tools you will use (Adobe, Descript, Opus Clip, Canva Pro)

- Grow with a team that values initiative, speed, and high standards



WHAT YOU WILL DO:



Short form (main focus):

- Take long form content (webinars, trainings, YouTube videos, podcasts) and turn it into short form clips for Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn

- Add attention-grabbing hooks, captions, emojis, B-roll, overlays, and pattern interrupts

- Create short testimonial videos from coaching calls, client interviews, and other raw footage

- Organize and manage video assets so clips are easy to find and reuse



Long form & channel support:

- Edit 1 piece of long form content per week (highlight key 
nts, add B-roll, overlays, and graphics)

- Publish that long form video on YouTube (upload, title, description, tags, end screens, cards)

- Create and upload custom YouTube thumbnails that drive clicks

- Handle one-off editing requests as needed (simple promos, clips, intros/outros, etc.)

- Analyze performance of published videos in YouTube Studio (CTR, views, watch time, retention) and provide simple reports and recommendations



Collaboration & cadence:

- Meet once a week with the owner of the company for feedback, direction, and priorities

- Join 1 monthly content planning session with the team

- Use AI tools (that we pay for) to speed up editing, transcription, captioning, and repurposing while still polishing by hand



Expected volume (part-time ~20 hours per week):

- 20–30 finished short form clips per week (30–90 seconds each)

- 1 edited and published long form YouTube video per week

- Ongoing testimonial edits and one-off requests as needed



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:



Experience & portfolio:

- Proven experience editing short form content from long form videos

- Strong portfolio showing:

Before/after edits from long form to short form

YouTube videos you have edited and thumbnails you have designed

Short testimonial / social proof videos (a plus)



Tool competence (we provide licenses):

You will work daily inside:

- Adobe Premiere Pro (primary editor)

- Basic Adobe After Effects (simple animations, lower thirds, transitions)

- Descript (transcription, rough cuts, repurposing)

- Opus Clip (AI clipping & short-form ideas)

- Canva Pro (YouTube thumbnails & simple graphics)

- YouTube Studio (publishing & analytics)

- Google Drive, Google Docs/Sheets (file management, tracking)

- ClickUp (task management)

- Slack (communication)



You don’t need to be a master of every tool on day one, but you must already be strong in at least one pro editor (Premiere / Final Cut / DaVinci) and be able to learn the rest quickly.

Having experience with video editing is a requirement to be considered for this role.

We will ask you what your favorite dog breed is, answer chowchow in your application so we know you have read the full job posting.

We will start with relatively limited hours (around 20 hours per week to begin) with the opportunity for it to grow as our relationship develops.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Mastery of the English language (spoken and written)

- Strong project management skills and ability to hit volume targets consistently

- Comfortable reviewing analytics and making basic performance recommendations

- Quiet environment to work



TECH REQUIREMENTS (MINIMUM):

- Computer: Windows 10/11 PC or Mac (released within the last 4–5 years)

CPU: Intel i7 / Ryzen 7 / Apple M1 or better (or equivalent)

RAM: Minimum 16 GB (32 GB strongly preferred)

Storage: At least 512 GB SSD, with 150+ GB free for active projects

GPU: Dedicated graphics card with 4 GB VRAM or more (e.g., NVIDIA GTX 1660 / RTX series or equivalent). For Mac, M1/M2 with integrated GPU is fine.



- Software capability:

Must be able to run Adobe Premiere Pro / After Effects smoothly while having multiple Chrome tabs, cloud drives, and AI tools (Descript / Opus Clip / etc.) open.



-High Speed Internet: Stable enough for uploading/downloading large video files and live calls without interruption

Reliable backup option (mobile data or secondary ISP) is a plus



- Other:

Quality headphones and microphone for edit reviews and team calls

Quiet, dedicated workspace suitable for focused editing work



If your current setup struggles with Adobe Premiere or AI tools like Descript / Opus Clip, please upgrade first before applying. This role requires fast, reliable hardware to hit our weekly volume targets.



SCHEDULE:

You must be available to work 4 hours per day, following this schedule: 3 PM – 7 PM Philippines time.



You must APPLY with this link:

title: Marketing Virtual Assistant
description:
About ALL DAY

ALL DAY is a specialty cafe in West End, Brisbane. We serve great coffee, a focused food menu, and we're actively growing a catering and events arm. We're a small, fast-moving team — we set clear priorities, move quickly, and expect the people we work with to do the same.



The Role

We're looking for a remote Marketing Manager to own ALL DAY's marketing, PR, and growth — end to end. This is a senior role that spans both traditional and digital marketing. You'll set the priorities, build the campaigns, and drive the outcomes without needing to be directed week to week.



Roughly 70% of your time will be on revenue-driving activity: traditional marketing, catering outreach, community and resident marketing,
 campaigns, PR, and media relations. About 15–20% is brand upkeep — social media, website, in-store materials, signage. The remaining 10–15% is light executive assistant support to the owner.



If you're at your best when you're executing a clear brief, this role isn't the right fit. We need someone who can look at the business, identify what will move the needle, and go after it without being prompted.



Core Responsibilities

Marketing & Growth

Own ALL DAY's marketing strategy across traditional and digital channels — set the priorities, build the plan, execute it

Lead catering outreach: prospect lists,
 campaigns, follow-ups, booking tracking

Drive community and resident marketing: welcome packs, local area campaigns, print collateral, real estate agent partnerships

Manage
 marketing end-to-end: list growth, campaigns, segmentation, performance reporting

Create and coordinate in-store marketing materials: posters, flyers, seasonal promotions, A-frame signage



Public Relations

Build and maintain relationships with local media, food writers, and relevant publications

Pitch ALL DAY stories, milestones, and menu updates to Brisbane press and lifestyle outlets

Identify, pitch, and coordinate influencer visits and collaborations

Manage listings on food guides, review platforms, and local directories

Track PR and influencer coverage and report on outcomes



Digital & Brand

Instagram and Google Business: consistent posting, captions, story content, review responses

Website (Squarespace): content updates, landing pages, SEO basics

Coordinate packaging and print suppliers



Executive Assistant Support (10–15% of time)

Research tasks, appointment booking, supplier follow-ups

File and task system management

General administrative/personal admin support to the owner as needed



What We're Looking For

Solid traditional and digital marketing experience — you've run campaigns across multiple channels

PR background or strong PR instincts — you know how to pitch a story and build media relationships

Strong written communication — you can write copy that's warm, clear, and commercially aware

Experience with
 marketing platforms (Mailchimp, Klaviyo, Mail Meteor, or similar)

You manage your own workload and don't need to be chased for updates

Hospitality or food & beverage experience is a genuine plus

Comfortable across Canva, Google Workspace, and Squarespace



Not the Right Fit If

You prefer waiting for a detailed task list before you can get started

Your background is primarily social media scheduling or admin work

You're not comfortable owning outcomes, only activities



How to Apply

Send your resume, a short cover note (3–5 sentences on why this role suits you), and at least one example or link to a campaign or project you've managed.



To confirm you've read the full brief, your subject line must start with: ALL DAY Ready — Marketing Manager Application. Applications without this exact subject line will not be reviewed.

title: Customer Service Rep (AI-Fluent Writer)
description:
The role



We run a US ecommerce brand and we need a customer service rep who writes like a human and uses AI like a pro. You'll answer customer
s, fix order problems, and turn angry shoppers into repeat buyers.



No macros. No copy-paste. You'll draft replies with ChatGPT or Claude, then rewrite them until they sound like a real person. If "AI fluency" means you opened ChatGPT once last year, this job isn't for you.



What you'll do



Handle 30 to 80 customer
s a day. Shipping questions, refund requests, product questions, complaints.

Draft replies with ChatGPT or Claude, then edit them until they sound human.

Fix order issues inside Shopify and CheckoutChamp. Refunds, address changes, cancellations and dispute management

Spot patterns. If five customers ask the same question in one week, tell us.

Keep the FAQ and canned response library updated as new questions come in.

Pitch in on light copy sometimes. Product blurbs, short
s, a social reply here and there.

You're a fit if you...



Write warm, natural English. No stiff "Dear valued customer" stuff.

Use ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini every single day. Not "I've heard of it."

Have at least one year of customer service experience, ideally in ecommerce or DTC.

Stay calm when customers yell in all caps.

Catch small things. Typos, wrong order numbers, tracking numbers that don't match the shipment.

Have reliable internet, a working computer, and a quiet spot to work.

Bonus points



You've used Shopify, Gorgias, Zendesk, Freshdesk, or Re:amaze.

You've worked in supplements, health, beauty, or pet products.

You can write light copy when we ask.



How to apply: the 4 gates



Copy-paste applications go straight in the trash. Clear all 4 gates below and we read yours first. Miss any and we skip you.



Gate 1. The subject line



Your
 subject has to be exactly this:



AI-CS | [Your First Name] | [Total number of bullet points in this entire post]



Count the bullets yourself, or paste this post into ChatGPT and ask. If you're AI-fluent, it takes 20 seconds. If your number is wrong, you're out.



Gate 2. The slop rewrite



Here's a real customer complaint and a bad AI-generated reply. Rewrite the reply the way a human with taste would send it.



Customer: "3 WEEKS and still no package. This is a joke. I want my money back and I'm leaving a 1-star review."



Bad AI reply (do NOT send as is): "Dear Valued Customer, I sincerely apologize for the inconvenience you've experienced with your recent order. Your satisfaction is our utmost priority and we deeply regret any frustration this situation may have caused. Please rest assured we are thoroughly investigating this matter and will do everything in our power to resolve it promptly. Thank you for your patience and for choosing our brand."



Send both:



The bad AI version (quoted)

Your rewrite

If your rewrite sounds like the bad one wearing a thesaurus costume, you fail this gate.



Gate 3. Prove you use AI



Paste a screenshot, or the full text, of a real ChatGPT or Claude conversation you had in the last 7 days. Any topic works. Recipes. Work tasks. Rants about your cat. We can tell when it's fake or from six months ago. People who actually use AI every day have dozens of fresh chats. People who don't have none.



Gate 4. The judgment trap



A customer writes:



"Your product didn't work for me. I bought it 90 days ago. I know your site says 60-day guarantee but I DEMAND a full refund or I'm disputing with my bank and reporting you to the BBB."



Write your reply in 2 to 3 sentences. There's no perfect answer. We're watching for empathy, spine, and care for the brand. Most people cave to the threat. Others go robotic. Do neither.



The last line



At the very bottom of your application, on its own line, write this:



I read the whole post.



If it's missing, we stop reading.

title: Senior Accountant (QuickBooks)
description:
Role Overview



We are looking for a Senior Accountant with hands-on experience in QuickBooks and full-cycle accounting. This role is responsible for managing the complete accounting process - from transaction recording to financial statement preparation. Experience in property or short-term rental accounting is a plus, but not required.



The ideal candidate has hands-on experience handling end-to-end accounting, reconciliations, and financial reporting, and can work independently with high accuracy.



Key Responsibilities



1. End-to-End Accounting

- Handle full-cycle accounting: journal entries, general ledger, and month-end close

- Maintain accurate and up-to-date financial records

- Ensure proper account classification and chart of accounts management



2. Financial Reporting

- Prepare monthly financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow)

- Perform variance analysis and explain key movements

- Ensure reports are accurate, timely, and aligned with management needs



3. Bank & Account Reconciliation

- Perform regular bank, credit card, and clearing account reconciliations

- Identify and resolve discrepancies promptly

- Maintain clean and reconciled books at all times



4. Accounts Payable & Receivable

- Manage vendor bills, payments, and expense tracking

- Monitor receivables and follow up on collections if needed

- Ensure proper documentation and approval processes



5. QuickBooks Management

- Maintain and manage QuickBooks Online (QBO)

- Ensure proper setup, integrations, and data accuracy

- Troubleshoot issues and support improvements in workflows



6. Compliance & Documentation

- Maintain organized records and audit-ready documentation

- Support tax filings and compliance requirements

- Follow and improve accounting processes and SOPs



Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting (CPA is a big plus)

- 5+ years of accounting experience with full-cycle accounting

- Strong experience with QuickBooks Online (QBO)

- Solid understanding of Financial statements, Reconciliations, General ledger and month-end close

- High attention to detail and ability to work independently



Nice to Have (Not Required)

- Experience in property, real estate, or short-term rental accounting

- Familiarity with tools like Guesty, AppFolio, or similar

- Experience with multi-entity accounting



To apply, send a link to your resume and relevant experience.



What We Offer

- 13th month pay

- HMO coverage

- Paid holidays

- Paid time off (PTO)

- Remote setup (US hours)

title: Remote Quantity Surveyor / Construction Estimator
description:
ABOUT US

Eagle Eye Estimations is a new Australian professional construction estimating business and we are excited to be growing. We provide detailed quantity take-offs and cost estimates for concrete and earthworks projects across residential, commercial, and civil sectors.

We are building our remote team now — which means you would be getting in at the ground floor with a business that is actively expanding. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with us long-term, not just fill a role.



THE ROLE

You will be preparing detailed estimates and quantity take-offs for concrete and earthworks projects. The role starts project-based with a clear pathway to a fixed ongoing arrangement as project volume grows.



RESPONSIBILITIES

– Perform quantity take-offs for concrete & earthworks

– Complete coloured measurement markups & legends in Bluebeam

– Create cut & fill heat maps using earthworks software (Kubla or similar)

– Prepare Bills of Quantities (BOQs) and pricing summaries

– Work with Australian engineering and architectural drawings

– Deliver accurate estimates within agreed deadlines



REQUIREMENTS

– Degree in Quantity Surveying, Civil Engineering, or similar (preferred)

– Minimum 3 years experience as a QS or Estimator in commercial or civil construction

– Strong skills in Bluebeam Revu

– Advanced Excel — formulas, structured take-off sheets

– Experience with earthworks software (Kubla, Mudshark, 12d, Civil 3D or similar)

– Ability to read and interpret engineering & architectural drawings

– Reliable internet connection and ability to meet deadlines

– Honest, proactive, and detail-oriented



PAY & GROWTH OPPORTUNITY

Eagle Eye Estimations is a new business and proud of it. Because we are in our early growth stage, this role currently pays per project based on complexity — meaning you are paid for every estimate you complete, with no lock-in on either side.

As project volume increases, we intend to move the right person into a fixed ongoing arrangement. If you want to be part of building something from the ground up and be rewarded for it as it grows, this is the role for you.



HOW TO APPLY — READ CAREFULLY

Do NOT apply through OnlineJobs. To apply, visit our skills assessment page:


/careers

Complete the online skills assessment in full. Only applicants who complete the assessment will be considered. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a live screen-share interview.

title: UI Prototype Engineer - FitForms + CloudTasker (Full Time, Remote)
description:
About the role



You will work directly with the founders of two separate SaaS platforms, FitForms and CloudTasker, both built for the health and fitness industry. Two distinct brands, two design systems, one role. You will be a core part of a small fast-moving product team supporting 7 software engineers who build directly from your output.

This is not a task-taking role. What you produce goes straight to the dev team. There is no design agency in the middle. Your work has to be production-ready.



About FitForms



FitForms is a member data capture platform built for boutique fitness studios. It has a strict design system, a defined component hierarchy, and an AI form builder called FitForms Agent. We are mid-rebuild on v2 with enterprise deals already in play.



About CloudTasker



CloudTasker is a business intelligence and action platform for health and fitness businesses. It gives studio owners live performance data, deep member insights, behaviour tracking, forecasting, CRM with live micro-contact views, and built-in communication tools including SMS,
, calling, and internal chat. It is a fast-growing product with a lot more on the horizon.

Two separate companies. Two separate brands. Both owned by the same founders. You will context-switch between them and keep the visual language of each product clean and distinct.



What this role actually is:



We do our product thinking in sessions with the founders and those decisions need to be turned into high-fidelity HTML prototypes that look and feel production-grade. Not wireframes. Not Figma. Functional, interactive, pixel-accurate HTML files that can be opened in a browser and demoed to enterprise clients, investors, and the dev team.

Your output is the handover artefact. Engineers build directly from your HTML prototypes. That means your code needs to be clean, your interactions need to be correct, and your interpretation of the brief needs to be accurate. Ambiguity gets flagged before you build, not after.

You will have access to a paid Claude plan to support your workflow. All communication and task management runs through ClickUp.



What you will be building across both products



FitForms:

	•	Builder canvas with panel layouts, field cards, canvas header, and status indicators

	•	Modal flows including the FitForms Agent AI builder with animation sequences

	•	Form state system: Draft, Published, and Inactive with correct visual treatment per state

	•	Interactive preview simulator in a device frame with phone and desktop toggle

	•	Onboarding flows including brand scrape preview

	•	Paid feature gates and upgrade experiences

	•	Enterprise UI patterns where locked elements simply do not render, not greyed out

	•	Full user workflows with annotated interaction notes for the engineering team



CloudTasker:

	•	Live performance dashboards with real time data layouts

	•	Member insight views and behaviour tracking interfaces

	•	CRM live views and micro-contact management screens

	•	Forecasting and business intelligence report layouts

	•	Communication interfaces including SMS,
, call, and internal chat

	•	Full user workflows with annotated interaction notes for the engineering team



What the FitForms design system looks like



Everything is tokens-based. You need to be able to hold a system in your head and apply it consistently.

	•	Primary brand: #E8473F

	•	AI gradient: #E8473F to #3D31C4 at 135 degrees, used exclusively for one feature

	•	Light mode panel hierarchy: #EEEAE6 / #FAFAF8 / #FFFFFF

	•	Dark mode: #0F0D0C through #363230

	•	Five-layer depth via tone, not colour

	•	Typography: Inter, 14px weight 500 for field names, 10px uppercase for labels

	•	Icon system: L
, 24px, stroke only, category-specific colours

CloudTasker has its own separate brand and design system. Full documentation will be provided on start.



What good looks like



	•	You read the brief fully and your first output is close

	•	You catch inconsistencies in the spec and flag them before building

	•	Your HTML is clean enough that an engineer can build directly from it

	•	You annotate interactions and states clearly so nothing needs to be interpreted

	•	You do not ship something that looks like a template

	•	You keep FitForms and CloudTasker visually distinct at all times

	•	You update your ClickUp tasks as you go without being asked



What you need



	•	Strong HTML and CSS including layout, depth, animation, and state management in vanilla JS or minimal framework

	•	An eye for detail that notices a 2px border-radius inconsistency

	•	Ability to read a written spec and translate it without hand-holding

	•	Comfort working from a written brief rather than Figma files

	•	Experience building product UI, not marketing sites, actual product interfaces

	•	Clear written communication in English, you will be working directly with founders daily

	•	Comfort using AI tools as part of your workflow, a paid Claude plan is provided

	•	Ability to context-switch between two distinct products and keep them visually separate



Bonus

	•	Experience prototyping SaaS builder interfaces, dashboards, or data-heavy UI

	•	Familiarity with the L
 icon set

	•	You have worked on a design system before and understand tokens

	•	Experience working with a software engineering team in a handover capacity



How we work



Briefs come through ClickUp with specs, design system tokens, and interaction requirements. You produce an HTML prototype, flag any questions before building, and update your task when the file is ready. We review together, iterate once or twice, and the file is handed to the engineering team. Turnaround on a standard screen is 24 to 48 hours.

This is a long term role. We move fast and we need someone who matches that pace and grows with both products.

13th month bonus paid in December.



Skills test



Before we move forward there is a short skills test. This takes most applicants 2 to 4 hours.

Build a single HTML file that replicates the FitForms canvas header exactly as described below.

The canvas header sits inside the canvas surface and contains:

	•	Form name, editable on click, Inter 14px weight 500, primary text colour #1C1917

	•	Form intent label below the form name, editable on click, 10px uppercase letter-spaced, secondary text colour #8A8480

	•	Undo and redo buttons on the left using L
 icons, 24px, stroke only, no fill, colour #8A8480

	•	A unified control group on the right containing:

	•	An auto-save indicator showing “Saved” in secondary text #8A8480, 12px

	•	A green “Live” status pill, small, rounded full

	•	An amber “Unpublished changes” pill, small, rounded full

	•	A Publish button, background #E8473F, white text, 32px height, 12px horizontal padding, Inter 13px weight 500



Layout requirements:

	•	The canvas background is #FFFFFF

	•	The page background behind the canvas is #FAFAF8

	•	The canvas has a visible drop shadow to create depth above the page background

	•	The canvas header sits at the top of the canvas with 16px padding on all sides

	•	The form name and intent label sit on the left, the control group sits on the right, all vertically centred

Use Inter from Google Fonts. No frameworks. Single file only.

If anything in the spec is unclear, list your questions before you start building. Do not guess and do not assume. Asking good questions before building is part of what we are testing.



To apply, send the following:



	1.	Three examples of HTML and CSS prototypes you have built. Product UI preferred, not landing pages. For each one tell us briefly what the brief was and what decisions you made.

	2.	Your completed skills test as a single .html file attachment.

No cover letter needed. No ZIP files. No Figma links. HTML file only for the test submission.

title: Direct Response Creative Editor (AI + UGC)

* Compensation

- Create word highlighting animations and adjust them to sync over singing performances.

- Take pre-livecut content and make adjustments to any missed timed cuts or switch

angles as needed. Add music, sound effects, transitions and graphics as instructed.

- Add graphic notation files to lessons and make sure they are in sync with what’s being

played.

- Keep track of multiple deliverables, both inside DaVinci Resolve and in spreadsheets or

management skills.



Requirements:



- Proficient in DaVinci with a personal account.

- High-speed reliable internet connection.

- Experience utilizing remote access workflows, such as Parsec or Jump Desktop.

- Experience using Slack for team communication.

- Experience using Asana or another task managing software.

- Prior experience in video editing, in projects that have multiple deliverables.

-  Strong organizational skills to keep track of multiple deliverables and keep NLE

project organization clear and concise.

- Strong collaborative and interpersonal skills.

- Open mindset with flexibility in workflow and release schedules.

- Excellent communication in English, both written and verbal.

- Ability to apply a strong working knowledge of music theory, rhythm, and

musical structure to edit music education content effectively, including:

        - Maintaining accurate timing and alignment with musical performances,

        - Supporting clarity of instruction through thoughtful visual pacing and cuts, 

       - Enhancing the learning experience for students through precise and

intentional editing choices.

title: Data Entry, Book Keeping & Recruiting Virtual Assistant
description:
Job Title

Operations & Recruiting Virtual Assistant (Bookkeeping & Financial Tracking)



Job Description

I run a fast-growing service business and I’m looking for a Virtual Assistant to take full ownership of my backend operations, financial tracking, and recruiting pipeline.

This role is responsible for maintaining accurate financial data, tracking margins and budgets, organizing company records, and managing the front end of hiring through Indeed.

All core systems are already in place, but they require some reorganization, cleanup, and ongoing maintenance.

You are not responsible for making business decisions. Your role is to execute systems, maintain accuracy, and keep all backend operations organized and up to date.



Responsibilities

Financial Tracking & Bookkeeping Support

Track all revenue, expenses, and subcontractor payouts

Maintain spreadsheets to monitor profit margins, job profitability, and overall business performance

Assist with budgeting and ensure spending is tracked accurately

Keep financial records clean, organized, and updated daily

Data Entry & Organization

Record job details including revenue, job scope, and subcontractor assignments

Ensure all company data is accurate, consistent, and easy to access

Maintain and update tracking sheets for business performance

Organize and maintain Google Drive and all internal files

Subcontractor Management (Data Only)

Maintain records for all subcontractors

Track payments and ensure accuracy of payouts

Keep clear documentation of all subcontractor activity

Indeed Recruiting Management

Monitor Indeed for new applicants throughout the day

Send initial messages to all applicants quickly using templates

Follow up with unresponsive candidates

Keep all applicants organized and updated

Initial Applicant Calls & Scheduling

Make short (5–7 minute) calls to applicants

Follow a script to confirm basic qualifications, explain the role briefly, and determine interest

Schedule qualified candidates into my calendar

Send confirmations and reminders to ensure candidates show up



Requirements

Strong understanding of basic bookkeeping and financial tracking

Comfortable working with spreadsheets (Google Sheets or Excel)

Strong written and spoken English

Highly organized and detail-oriented

Comfortable making phone calls daily

Ability to follow systems and instructions exactly

Previous experience with bookkeeping, admin work, or recruiting is preferred



Compensation

$4–$8/hour depending on experience

30–40 hours per week

Opportunity for long-term role based on performance



Success Metrics

All systems are already in place and must be kept organized, accurate, and up to date

Systems should be cleaned up, improved, and maintained consistently

Financial data is tracked clearly and correctly

Subcontractor payments and job data remain accurate

All files and records are easy to access and well organized

Recruiting pipeline is consistently moving with fast response times

Qualified candidates are scheduled efficiently with a high show-up rate



Important

You are not responsible for making financial decisions or hiring decisions. Your role is to execute systems, maintain organization, and ensure all data is accurate and up to date.

title: Creative Video Producer
description:
We're a New Zealand based company running four brands: an AI consulting firm, a Shopify SEO agency, an insurance brokerage, and a personal brand. Our audience is mostly Australia and New Zealand.



We're hiring a creative video producer to own video production across all four brands. This is a full-time, fully remote role. We're based in Auckland, NZ but you can work from anywhere with reliable internet.



You'll take rough concepts, reference videos, and raw footage and turn them into finished content. Around 15-20 videos per month across:



- Short-form for LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

- Long-form YouTube videos

- Talking head edits of real footage

- AI UGC (AI avatars, AI voice)

- Podcast clips

- B-roll and text-driven content



You pick the right approach per video. We don't micromanage.



- Full tool stack (paid by us): CapCut, HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Kling, Claude, ChatGPT, Captions, and anything else you need

Training on any tools you haven't used before

- Creative freedom. We send rough ideas, you bring them to life

- Fully remote, work the hours that suit you. We care about output, not hours logged

- Full-time, long-term contract with a monthly retainer



What We Need You to Be Good At



- Writing scripts that sound like a real person, not AI slop. This is the most important skill. You'll use AI heavily (we provide the tools), but you need to know how to prompt, edit, and shape the output so it sounds human.

- Creative judgment. We send a rough idea, you come back with something better than we asked for.

-  Range. Short-form hooks, long-form YouTube pacing, talking head edits, AI-assisted production. Not a one-trick editor.

- Taste for ANZ and Western audiences. 



What We Need in Your Application



At least 3 to 5 videos you created yourself. Not client edits. Not executions of someone else's brief. Work where you owned the concept, wrote the script, and produced it end to end.



If you don't have this, don't apply.



How to Apply



- Complete this application form: 

- It takes 20-30 minutes. Read every question carefully. Generic or rushed answers will be rejected.

Important: Include the word Mustang somewhere in your application so we know you actually read this post. Applications without it will be ignored.

- Only completed forms are reviewed.

- Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a paid test task.

title: Web Developer
description:
About Premium Invention

We're a profitable DTC brand selling premium solar-powered security cameras ($
) to off-grid customers across North America — ranchers, cabin owners, farmers, construction sites. We sell directly to consumers through Shopify Plus. We're launching multiple new products in 2026 and need a developer who can keep our storefronts fast, tested, and converting.



What You'll Actually Do:

Our Shopify Plus setup spans four subdomains (US, Canada, UK, Australia) with ongoing CRO tests, product launches, and

checkout optimization. Day-to-day, you'll be:

- Building and iterating on landing pages for new product launches

- Implementing CRO tweaks from our Head of Growth and Product Marketing team (A/B tests, new sections, trust elements, comparison tables)

- Debugging and fixing issues across all four regional storefronts

- Managing DNS, subdomains, and domain configurations

- Optimizing site speed — we lose revenue every time a page crawls

- Checkout customization within Shopify Plus Scripts and Functions

- Using Claude Code / Cursor to move faster, not as a crutch

- You'll work closely with our marketing team under the Head of Growth. You're not just taking tickets — you're flagging what's broken, suggesting better implementations, and pushing back when something won't work.



Must-Have:

3+ years professional development experience with Shopify Plus specifically (not just basic Shopify)

Hands-on with Liquid, HTML, CSS, JavaScript — you write this code yourself, not just direct others

DNS and domain management — you've set up and debugged subdomains before

Clear written and spoken English — team coordination happens over Zoom

Comfortable with AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) — we work fast and expect you to leverage these



Nice to Have:

Experience with Shopify Plus Scripts and Shopify Functions

Built custom checkout experiences on Plus

Worked on DTC brands selling physical products (not just agency work)

Familiar with tools like Hotjar, GA4, Google Tag Manager

Experience with multi-region / multi-currency Shopify Plus setups



Not For You If:

Your Shopify experience is only Basic/Advanced Shopify, not Plus

Your experience is limited to Shopify theme customization without backend or Plus-specific work

You need detailed specs for every task and can't work from a brief

You're looking for a full-time salaried role right now (this starts part-time)

You're uncomfortable being tracked via Hubstaff while actively working

AI coding tools aren't already part of your daily workflow



How to Apply

We do not accept applications through OnlineJobs.ph messaging. To be considered, please complete our application form here:


/?k=SfRyU1Cmbk2mpPc4MziuJw&d=
The form takes 20-25 minutes and includes a required Loom video — strong candidates will be invited to an interview within 24 hours.

title: Appointment Setter for U.S. Life Insurance Agency — Base + Bonus Per Appt ($600-$2,000/mo potential) — LONG TERM
description:
Hi future rockstar 

I run a life insurance agency in Florida (Globe Life — American Income Division) and I'm looking to build a small, tight team of 2–3 top-tier appointment setters in the Philippines to call warm leads in the U.S.



This is NOT a cold-calling boiler room. These are warm leads — real Americans who filled out a form online asking about life insurance for their family. Your job is simple: call them back, confirm they're interested, and book them onto my calendar. I do the closing.



If you're fast, coachable, have clear English, and you actually want to build a career instead of bouncing between gigs — read on.



WHAT YOU'LL EARN



Base: $3–$6/hr depending on experience (paid weekly via Wise / PayPal)

Bonus: Paid per appointment that shows up to the call with me

Closer bonus: Additional bonus if the appointment turns into a closed sale

Top performer bonus: Weekly + monthly

Realistic monthly earnings: $600–$1,200/mo starting. Top performers hit $1,500–$2,500/mo consistently.

13th month + paid time off after 6 months

Annual raise based on performance



This is a long-term role. I'm not looking for someone for 2 months. I want to train you, pay you well, and grow with you for years.



WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO



Call warm U.S. leads using our power dialer (auto-dials for you — no manual dialing)

Follow our proven script (7 steps, I'll train you on it day 1)

Confirm interest, light qualifying, book them onto my Google Calendar

Send SMS confirmations + reminders (we have templates)

Log every call in the CRM with a disposition code

Submit end-of-day report on Slack

Attend daily 10-min huddle + weekly 1-on-1 coaching



~50–80 dials per hour. 3–8 appointments per shift is the target.



 MUST HAVE



Very clear spoken English — Americans must understand you easily. Neutral/light accent preferred.

Minimum 1 year BPO, appointment setting, or sales call experience

Stable fiber internet — 25+ Mbps (NO mobile hotspots, NO unstable connections)

Noise-cancelling USB headset (not a gaming one, not laptop mic)

Backup power (UPS) — brownouts cannot stop your shift

Quiet dedicated workspace — no roosters, no karaoke neighbor, no kids running around during calls

Willing to work U.S. hours — most shifts run 2 PM – 11 AM Manila time (this is U.S. daytime)

Laptop/desktop less than 3 years old

Tech-savvy enough to learn a CRM, Google Calendar, Calendly and 
 in 2–3 days





 NICE TO HAVE (not required, but moves you to the top of the pile)



Previous experience selling insurance, final expense, or mortgage leads to U.S. customers

Experience with Go High Level, Wavv, or similar dialers

Coachable, humble, and hungry — willing to be QA'd daily for the first 2 weeks





 PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF



Your English is not crystal clear on the phone

You're looking for a "part-time side thing" — I need commitment

You can't work U.S. hours (I pay a night differential — it's worth it)

You've never done phone work before

You get nervous on calls or freeze under pressure

You see this as a stepping stone — I want long-term teammates





 HOW TO APPLY (READ CAREFULLY — this is how I filter)

Applications that don't follow these steps will be ignored. This isn't me being difficult — it's me filtering for people who actually read instructions. That skill matters in this job.

Put this exact phrase in your cover letter subject line:



I READ THE POST — ABIN SAJI 2026



In your application, send me:



A 60-second voice recording (Vocaroo link works great) reading this out loud in your most natural "phone voice":





"Hey John, this is Sam calling from Globe Life, American Income Division. You recently requested some information on life insurance coverage for your family — does that ring a bell? Awesome — I'm not the agent, I'm just the one who gets you scheduled with our licensed underwriter in your state. It'll only take me 2–3 minutes to confirm a few things and get you on the calendar. Fair enough?"





A short paragraph (3–5 sentences) answering: What's the biggest objection you've ever overcome on a call, and how?

A screenshot of your internet speed test (speedtest.net)

Your expected monthly rate (base only, not including bonus)

Your available start date





 HIRING PROCESS



Week 1: I review applications and invite the top 10 to a Zoom interview

Week 2: Top 3 do a paid 2-hour role-play / mock call session

Week 3: Top 1–2 start paid training

Month 2: If you hit minimum KPIs ? you're on the team for the long haul





 WHY WORK WITH ME

I'm not a faceless agency. I'm a working agent who built this team to scale my own book. That means:



You talk directly to the owner (me), not 3 layers of managers

Feedback loops are fast — I can change scripts, fix tools, raise pay instantly

I actually reward performance — top producers make real money here

I invest in training. You'll leave this job a better setter than you came in, guaranteed.

I'm looking for 2–3 people. If that's you — apply today. I review every application personally.



Salam / Kumusta,

Abin Saji

Globe Life — American Income Division

Orlando, FL, US

title: Operations Assistant (AU Accounting Support)
description:
Operations Assistant (AU Accounting Support)



We are looking for a process-driven Operations Assistant with a strong background in the Australian (AU) Accounting landscape. You’ll be the "engine room" of our practice, ensuring that our workflows are seamless, our data is pristine, and our team remains accountable to deadlines.



If you thrive on organizing chaos, mastering practice management software, and ensuring no BAS lodgment or onboarding task falls through the cracks, we want to hear from you.



Your Core Responsibilities



- Operational Structure & Workflow: Maintain and optimize the internal architecture of our practice to ensure maximum efficiency.

- Workflow Visibility: Ensure all tea
mbers and stakeholders have a clear, real-time view of project statuses and upcoming deadlines.

- Task Accountability: Monitor task assignments and follow up with tea
mbers to ensure milestones are met on time.

- Time Tracking Compliance: Oversee team time-logs to ensure accurate reporting for capacity planning and client billing.

Qualifications & Skills

mbers to ensure accountability.
